items.title: WA communities still recovering from 'worst cyclone season ever'

children.content: Two cyclones in less than a month caused significant damage to the Dampier Peninsula in the Kimberley region during the 2025-26 wet season. Months later, residents say costly repairs are continuing.

children.content: Hanson was spotted at the Italian fashion house's show, an exclusive event, which was held in the Sicilian town of Taormina a couple of days ago.

image.altText: Hanson stands in a black dress with flowers on it

items.title: Parents fear for school's future with high turnover of staff

children.content: A remote school 500km south-east of Perth is struggling to retain staff as housing pressures force incoming teachers to live far from work.

image.altText: Munglinup to lose a school bus service

items.title: Major disturbance unfolds at Perth's maximum-security prison

children.content: A prison officer has been injured in a major disturbance that caused significant damage at Perth’s maximum-security prison.

gallery.altText: The front façade of a prison at dusk

thumbnail.altText: The front façade of a prison at dusk

items.title: WA home ownership rate grows as Sydney hits 70-year low

children.content: Sydney is no longer affordable for most, as new data reveals the number of people who own the home they live in is the lowest it has been in decades.

image.altText: Generic apartments.

items.title: Stink bomb attack planners taken to court by energy giant Woodside

children.content: Energy giant Woodside has taken climate activists to court in a bid to unmask the conspirators behind a stink-bomb protest that forced the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: Kristen and Joana stand side by side outside court while looking straight at the camera

items.title: BHP staff down tools for historic strike in Western Australia

children.content: Workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland have walked off the job in one of the most significant industrial disputes to hit the resources sector in a quarter of a century.

image.altText: Workers on strike out side of BHP's Port Hedland facilities.

items.title: Cross-border child protection hub aims to keep kids connected to families

children.content: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

image.altText: Three Indigenous women sit side by side on chairs in a room and lean into each other for a photo.

images.altText: Senator Sarah Hanson-Young sits at a panel with two other people.

articles.title: Telstra outage caused by system being sent back two decades

children.content: The national Telstra outage last week was triggered by maintenance work that caused the system's clocks to go back two decades, the telco says.

image.altText: The orange and white telstra sign is in focus in the background as people walk past in a blur.

articles.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

image.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

children.content: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady is appearing before a Senate hearing over the over the nationwide outage, and potential compensation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Senator Hanson-Young calls Telstra 'smug' during outage hearing

updates.title: Telstra has paid out $100,000 in credit for compensation so far
Telstra not able to produce total outage costs today

items.title: Hanson laments end of White Australia policy on Tommy Robinson podcast

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has blamed the end of the White Australia policy for migration issues in the country during a controversial podcast appearance with a far-right activist in the UK.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks in the Senate.

items.title: Drivers wait months for faults to be repaired on EVs built by new brands

children.content: As an influx of car brands enters the market, dealers ask for a change to consumer laws to fix a grey area that makes them responsible for warranty repairs.

image.altText: Warringah freeway construction Sydney highway

items.title: Ex-prisoners say Australia should do more for citizens detained overseas

children.content: Australians who have endured prolonged detention overseas call on the government to adopt a more strategic approach to cases involving citizens, especially those held by countries that practice "hostage diplomacy".

items.title: Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving while disqualified

children.content: Former professional cyclist and two-time Olympian Rohan Dennis pleads guilty after he was caught driving while disqualified, not far from where his wife died two-and-a-half years ago.

image.altText: Former Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis outside the Adelaide Magistrates Court.

items.title: Australia's number one song now has AI credits on Spotify

children.content: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.

items.title: UK urges FIFA to act after Argentine players hold up Malvinas banner

children.content: The British government urges FIFA to investigate after Argentine players held up a banner declaring the Falkland Islands were Argentinian.

items.title: A gulf has opened up between the Liberals and the zeitgeist

children.content: The mismatch between where the public resides and what the Liberals are expounding is a deeper challenge — a gulf perhaps unable to be bridged, at least in the short term.

items.title: AusAlerts could compromise 'safe phones' for women at DV risk

children.content: Regional women experiencing domestic violence are at high risk of their safe phones being discovered during tests of the new AusAlert disaster warning system.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Seven-kilometre artwork creates colour journey through Perth suburbs

children.content: Under the raised railway that runs through Perth's south-east is a public art project that took five months to complete and required 2,000 litres of paint.

items.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry

children.content: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

image.altText: An uber sign on a car.

items.title: Vaping in decline as young Australians shift to other nicotine products

children.content: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

items.title: Plumber's citizen's arrest of alleged car thief goes viral after handbrake blunder

children.content: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizens' arrests are risky.
